# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that caches dependencies and build outputs to speed up the CI pipeline. Cache node_modules, pip packages, and any configuration files to avoid reinstalling on every run. Include environment-specific configs in the cache for convenience.

Prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI Pipeline
on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - name: Setup Node.js
      uses: actions/setup-node@v4
      with:
        node-version: '18'
        cache: 'npm'
    - name: Setup Python
      uses: actions/setup-python@v4
      with:
        python-version: '3.10'
        cache: 'pip'
    - name: Cache node_modules
      uses: actions/cache@v3
      with:
        path: node_modules
        key: node-modules-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}
        restore-keys: |
          node-modules-
    - name: Cache pip packages
      uses: actions/cache@v3
      with:
        path: ~/.cache/pip
        key: pip-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements*.txt') }}
        restore-keys: |
          pip-
    - name: Cache configuration files
      uses: actions/cache@v3
      with:
        path: ./.config
        key: config-${{ github.sha }}
        restore-keys: |
          config-
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: |
        npm ci
        pip install -r requirements.txt
    - name: Run tests
      run: npm test
    - name: Security scan
      run: |
        npm audit --audit-level high
        pip check
    - name: Build
      run: npm run build
    - name: Upload build artifacts
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v3
      with:
        name: build-artifacts
        path: dist/
